title: Senators Marshall and Klobuchar Lead Bipartisan, Bicameral Legislation on Biofuel Tax Credit
content: U.S. Senators Roger Marshall, M.D. (R-Kansas) and Amy Klobuchar (D-Minnesota) today reintroduced the bipartisan and bicameral Farmer First Fuel Incentives Act, which would restrict the eligibility of the 45Z Tax Credit to renewable fuels made only from domestically sourced feedstocks. U.S. Representatives Tracey Mann (R-Kansas-01) and Marcy Kaptur (D-Ohio-09) have introduced an identical bill in the House of Representatives. The CFPC that was set to go into effect on Jan. 1, 2025  would be extended to 2034 from its current sunset in 2027.
author: Analysts Account
